title: Sarcocornia dunensis
uses: Sarcocornia dunensis, a halophytic plant species, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Research suggests that it may possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which could be beneficial in treating various health conditions. However, more extensive studies are required to fully understand its medicinal uses. Some studies have also investigated its potential in treating skin conditions, such as eczema, due to its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential applications in medicine.
taxonomy: Sarcocornia dunensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaranthaceae. It is a halophytic shrub that belongs to the genus Sarcocornia. Sarcocornia dunensis is often classified as Sarcocornia in the APG III system of plant taxonomy. The species is native to coastal areas of Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. In some classifications, it is considered a synonym of Sarcocornia perennis.

side_effects: Sarcocornia dunensis, also known as marsh samphire, is a plant that is generally considered safe for consumption.
